analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet celebrates the start of COVID-19 vaccinations across the EU, highlighting simultaneous rollout in multiple capitals, prioritization of the vulnerable, and assurance of sufficient doses for everyone. Evaluation: EU1 (successful story) – Implicitly yes: it portrays a milestone achievement, suggesting effective joint action and progress, but the emphasis is more on present unity and delivery than historical success. EU2 (united) – Yes: references coordinated vaccination "everywhere across the EU" and cites multiple cities, conveying solidarity and joint action. EU3 (useful for its citizens) – Yes: stresses protecting the vulnerable and ensuring enough doses for all, showing practical benefits of EU-level action to citizens. EU4 (avant-garde) – Not clearly: it does not claim pioneering leadership or regulatory innovation, only implementation. EU5 (global champion of fair causes) – Not applicable. EU6 (contributes to a better world) – Not applicable; focus is internal, not global. Conclusion: The tweet promotes EU2 and EU3; EU1 is arguable but secondary and not explicit.
